title: JNC 8: Eighth Joint National Committee Hypertension Guidelines
content: For general population aged ≥60 years, initiate pharmacological treatment at SBP ≥150 mm Hg or DBP ≥90 mm Hg. For general population <60 years, initiate at DBP ≥90 mm Hg. For patients with CKD or diabetes, initiate at SBP ≥140 mm Hg or DBP ≥90 mm Hg. First-line agents: thiazide diuretics, CCBs, ACE inhibitors, or ARBs (non-black); thiazide diuretics or CCBs preferred for black patients. Treatment goal: SBP <150 mm Hg (≥60 years) or <140 mm Hg (<60 years).

title: ADA Standards of Medical Care in Diabetes 2024
content: Glycaemic targets: HbA1c <7% for most non-pregnant adults. Less stringent goal (HbA1c <8%) appropriate for patients with limited life expectancy, advanced complications, or hypoglycaemia unawareness. More stringent goal (HbA1c <6.5%) for selected patients if achievable without significant hypoglycaemia. Fasting plasma glucose target: 80-130 mg/dL; postprandial peak <180 mg/dL. Hypoglycaemia level 1: glucose <70 mg/dL; level 2 (clinically significant): <54 mg/dL; level 3 (severe): cognitive impairment requiring third-party assistance.

title: AHA/ACC Heart Rate Management in Atrial Fibrillation
content: Rate control target in AF: resting heart rate <110 bpm (lenient strategy) acceptable if patient is asymptomatic and preserved EF. Strict rate control (<80 bpm at rest, <110 bpm during 6-minute walk) for persistent symptoms. Bradycardia threshold: HR <60 bpm at rest in ambulatory patients may indicate sinus node dysfunction. HR >150 bpm at rest with symptoms: assess for SVT, flutter, or AF with rapid ventricular response. HR >200 bpm: immediate clinical evaluation required.

title: KDIGO CKD: Chronic Kidney Disease Clinical Practice Guideline
content: GFR categories: G1 ≥90, G2 60-89, G3a 45-59, G3b 30-44, G4 15-29, G5 <15 (kidney failure). Serum creatinine alone is insufficient to stage CKD; eGFR calculation (CKD-EPI equation) required. Creatinine >2.0 mg/dL in adults typically corresponds to eGFR <50 mL/min/1.73m². Refer to nephrology: eGFR <30 mL/min/1.73m² (G4/G5), rapidly progressive decline (>5 mL/min/year), or AKI superimposed on CKD.

title: WHO Sepsis-3 and qSOFA Clinical Criteria
content: Sepsis-3 definition: life-threatening organ dysfunction caused by dysregulated host response to infection (SOFA score increase ≥2). qSOFA (quick-SOFA) criteria for bedside identification: (1) RR ≥22/min; (2) altered mentation (GCS <15); (3) SBP ≤100 mm Hg. Presence of ≥2 qSOFA criteria associated with 3-14x increase in in-hospital mortality. Septic shock: vasopressor requirement to maintain MAP ≥65 mm Hg with serum lactate >2 mmol/L despite adequate fluid resuscitation.

title: IDSA/ATS Community-Acquired Pneumonia Severity (CURB-65)
content: CURB-65 severity scoring for CAP: Confusion (new), Urea >7 mmol/L (BUN >19 mg/dL), RR ≥30/min, BP (SBP <90 or DBP ≤60), Age ≥65 years. Score 0-1: outpatient treatment; score 2: inpatient; score 3-5: ICU consideration. SpO2 <92% on room air: supplemental oxygen indicated; SpO2 <88%: evaluate for NIV or mechanical ventilation. RR ≥30/min is an independent predictor of 30-day mortality in pneumonia.

title: KDIGO Acute Kidney Injury Clinical Practice Guideline
content: AKI definition: increase in serum creatinine ≥0.3 mg/dL within 48 hours; or ≥1.5x known/presumed baseline within 7 days; or urine output <0.5 mL/kg/h for ≥6 hours. AKI staging — Stage 1: creatinine 1.5-1.9x baseline; Stage 2: 2.0-2.9x baseline; Stage 3: ≥3.0x baseline or creatinine ≥4.0 mg/dL or initiation of RRT. Hyperkalaemia management in AKI: K+ >6.5 mEq/L with ECG changes requires emergent treatment (calcium gluconate, insulin/glucose, kayexalate or dialysis).

title: WHO Anaemia Classification and Management
content: WHO anaemia thresholds: Men ≥15 years: Hgb <13 g/dL; Women ≥15 years (non-pregnant): Hgb <12 g/dL; pregnant women: Hgb <11 g/dL. Severity classification: Mild 10.0-11.9 g/dL (women)/12.9 g/dL (men); Moderate 8.0-9.9 g/dL; Severe <8.0 g/dL. Transfusion trigger (general): Hgb <7 g/dL in stable non-cardiac patients; <8 g/dL in cardiac disease or post-operative. Hgb <6 g/dL: symptomatic transfusion almost always indicated.
